content:
INT. DETMER RESIDENCE - ANDREW'S ROOM
 The room is dingy. Unkempt. The camera sits on the bed, on its side, facing the door. We can hear someone moving around off screen.
 The door handle clicks; someone's trying it.
 Then nothing. Then, suddenly, loud pounding on the door.
 Andrew's voice is scratchy and prone to cracking.
 He speaks with a rushed mix of fear and anxiety.
O.S.) What do you want, I'm getting ready for school-

MR. DETMER (O.S.) Why is the door locked, unlock this fucking door right now.
 The bed stirs as Andrew sits down.
 MR. DETMER (O.S.) I said unlock this door. UNLOCK THE DOOR. OPEN THE DOOR, NOW.
O.S.) You're drunk-
MR. DETMER (O.S.) Listen, you don't tell me- IF I'M DRUNK, OR-
O.S.) It's seven thirty. In the AM. You're drunk, dad, that's crazy-
MR. DETMER (O.S.) What're you doing in there.
O.S.) I'm filming this.
MR. DETMER (O.S.) What?
O.S.) I bought a camera. I'm filming all your shit from now on. 2.

INT. DETMER RESIDENCE - SANDRA DETMER'S ROOM
 Equally dingy. SANDRA DETMER, gaunt and sickly, is sat up in bed. Andrew's filming her. She's clearly very ill, speaking in a weak rasp. Andrew now holds the camera.
O.S.) Mom? Will you say hi to the camera?
Who's the audience?
O.S.) The millions of people watching at home.
Hello world. Do I look awful?
O.S.) No, you look great.
I've been looking a little better, yeah?
O.S.) Oh yeah, definitely.
O.S.) Oh, yeah, thanks.

CUT.
 They're driving.
So...Should I ask about the camera, or-
O.S.) I'm filming things now. I'm filming everything.
You're filming everything.
O.S.) For my mom. I'm trying to get custody of her from my dad. She's getting worse, and he's not...helping, and this way, in case something goes down-
He gets violent or whatever-
O.S.) Right, it'd be evidence.
Evidence. But you're not with him right now, but you're filming this.
O.S.) Well, yeah, to add context.

Context.
 Andrew, you are...a weird dude.
CUT.
Did you ever read any Auguste Comte?
O.S.) What is that?
O.S.) For school?
He's just like- his whole thing is about being positive and like, taking up for yourself. You should read him, maybe, it might make you feel- you know, improve your outlook.
O.S.) Yeah, right.

EXT. BENJAMIN FRANKLIN HIGH - PARKING LOT
 Andrew's getting out of the car, but then ducks back in to see Matt lighting a pipe.
O.S.) Are you not coming in?
I'm gonna blaze a little first, yeah?
O.S.) You're going to miss first period-
Matt turns on the radio, loud.
O.S.) Okay, okay.

INT. BENJAMIN FRANKLIN HIGH - HALLWAY
 Andrew's filming himself putting stuff in his locker, and does a quick sweep of the crowded school hallway.

O.S.) This is my school, I guess.
 This is the hallway-
O.S.) ...yeah-

GIRL Every vote counts.
 Andrew films the flyer for a moment, brightly colored and featuring a picture of a smiling Steve Kazinsky, before something yanks the camera away.
 For the first time we see ANDREW Detmer, 17, pale, awkward and gangly, with long, stringy hair and thin, scraggly beard. He looks anxious, if not afraid.
 BRYCE (O.S.) Yo this camera is a piece of shit. It's like from 2004 or some shit.
 WAYNE, 17, big and hateable in his Ed Hardy T-shirt, appears wrapping his arm roughly around Andrew.
 WAYNE Hey, how do I look? (starts muscle posing) Like this? Ooh, that's good.
 Like this? That's sexy, right?
Bryce, gimme my camera back-

BRYCE (O.S.) Fuck you Andrew, shut up.
 This camera's a piece of shit.
 WAYNE You got me, let's go. Wayne turns and knocks everything out of Andrew's locker. Bryce starts to walk away with the camera.
O.S.) Hey, Bryce, come on, give it back-
The camera is set down on the ground, and then abruptly kicked back to Andrew. He picks it up, checking on it.
(quietly, sad) Oh come on... 6.

EXT. BENJAMIN FRANKLIN HIGH - FOOTBALL FIELD
 A view from the bleachers as the soccer team practices. The cheerleaders are practicing too. We cut: a different view, lower.
O.S.) This is where I eat lunch, out here on the bleachers.

CUT.
 LATER.
 The camera's next to Andrew as he's eating, down on the bench. A CHEERLEADER approaches.
 CHEERLEADER Hi.
Hey-
CHEERLEADER Could you not videotape us, please? It's really fucking creepy.
I wasn't, videotaping you, so much as I was just-

CHEERLEADER
 ANDREW Just don't videotape-
 -you know, filming what I do during the-
 CHEERLEADER Don't videotape us, okay, or we'll call security. We see you watching us, we're not stupid, and it's sketchy, so back off.
okay.
CHEERLEADER Is it on right now?
Yes.

INT. MATT'S CAR
 Matt's driving.
 Andrew's filming from the passenger seat.

There's a party tonight.
 A barn party at Haven Hills.
O.S.) I thought Haven Hills was closed.
It's abandoned, yeah. That's why it's a good place for a party. Two kegs.
O.S.) Why are you telling me?
You wanna go? I don't wanna go alone.
When was the last time you went to a party?
O.S.) I don't like parties.

You're a senior.
 Just come, you'll have fun. ANDREW (O.S.) I'll think about it.
 Okay, right. Andrew, can I give you like, a pro tip?
Yeah?

Keep the camera at home.
 It's weird.
It has a purpose- 8.

INT. DETMER RESIDENCE - ANDREW'S ROOM
 The camera lays on Andrew's bed again, filming the room. Andrew is on his laptop at a desk, working.
I'm uploading what I shot today...you have to keep a back-up, you know.

EXT. HAVEN HILLS FARM - NIGHT
 We're in Matt's car, driving on a dark dirt road, past an old fence and rusted out farm equipment.
O.S.) Are you sure it's here? (beat) Are you sure it's here, this all looks closed-

It's here, calm down.
 Listen.
We can faintly hear party music.
O.S.) Oh yeah.

EXT. HAVEN HILLS FARM - BARN
 Cars are parked outside a big abandoned barn as Matt and Andrew approach. We see Christmas lights strung up, and there's clearly a party going on inside the barn. Kids are milling about outside, too, drinking, fucking around.
Hey dude, don't follow me around all night.
O.S.) What?
Don't follow me around all night, okay?
O.S.) What am I supposed to do? I thought you said you didn't want to go alone- 10.

Talk to people?
 Have a beer, I don't know.

MONTAGED FOOTAGE...
 Andrew skulks around the party, which is clearly in full swing. Lots of bad footage of people talking, drinking, girls dancing sexy, guys joking around. Andrew is silent and detached, just filming. He sees a girl, CASEY Letter, 16, filming as well. They shout over the music.
Hey, what are you filming for?
O.S.) Oh, I don't- just filming.

What?
 I'm filming for my blog!
O.S.) What?

I have a video blog!
 Why are you filming?

Andrew simply backs away. More footage of the party. AUSTIN, 18, in an Ed Hardy shirt, comes out of nowhere.
 He's clearly a little drunk.
 AUSTIN Hey, were you filming my girlfriend? Yes or no, were you filming my girlfriend?
O.S.) No, I wasn't filming anyone specific, just filming the party.
AUSTIN (beat) Okay, good, because I don't wanna have to beat your ass.
O.S.) Okay.
AUSTIN Don't talk back to me. Don't get smart, okay, I'm being nice here. I'm being nice to you. (MORE) 11. AUSTIN (beat) Say okay.
O.S.) Okay-

EXT. HAVEN HILLS FARM - EDGE OF THE WOODS
 Andrew's out alone in a field now, furiously cleaning the lens of the camera, obstructing our view. Cut to a few moments later; the camera now resting in his lap or on the ground. We can see the barn, the party still going on.
 After a few moments, we can faintly hear Andrew crying.
O.S.) (loud, sudden) Are you Andrew?
O.S.) (startled) What- STEVE Are you Andrew-with-the-camera? (beat) Matt's cousin, Matt said you had a camera?

The camera is picked up to reveal STEVE "Kaz" Kazinksy, 17, approachably handsome and in great shape, with an easygoing, instantly likeable charisma.
 He's a little drunk and giddy.
Dude, are you okay?
O.S.) I'm Andrew. 12.
I'm Steve Kazinsky-
O.S.) No, I know.
yeah?
O.S.) Everybody knows who you are.
Haha, yes, that is true. Listen, come on, bring the camera.

O.S.) What?
 I don't-
Come on.
Steve starts off, but Andrew hesitates.
O.S.) I don't-

EXT. HAVEN HILLS FARM - FIELD
 Steve is walking ahead in the field of tall grass, toward woods. It's rather dark. ANDREW (O.S.) Look, I don't think we're supposed to-

Is there a light on that?
 On your camera?
O.S.) Oh, I don't- Hm, I don't know-
They've reached the woods, and Steve is climbing over the remains of a fence. The light bounces around wildly.
O.S.) What were you guys doing out here? This is way out-
Me and your cousin were blazing a little.
O.S.) Matt's out here?
A bunch of people were out here, but I think they've all gone back, now, cause we're like, obsessed with the thing.
O.S.) The what?

CUT.
 They're in the woods now, thick and dizzying. It's near silent but their feet crushing the Fall leaves on the ground. Steve walks out ahead.

Andrew Detmer, that's right.
 I remember you from homeroom, Freshman year.

O.S.) You remember that? STEVE Yeah, I have a memory for faces. I'm gonna go into politics, which is ironic because I'm soooo fucking high right now- probably shouldn't be letting you videotape this-
 O.S.) I'm gonna be so rich when you're president, you have no idea-
O.S.) Down here!
O.S.) Matt?

The camera turns to reveal Matt, standing next to some kind of craggy hole in the ground, at the base of a hill in the woods. It's big, but the darkness beyond is impenetrable to the camera. It looks like a big black spot.
There we go. There it is.

CUT.
 Andrew and Steve are now down with Matt by the hole. Andrew is keeping the camera fixed squarely on the murky blackness, nervous.
V.O.) Where is everybody?
They all left.
O.S.) We're not supposed to be out here.
Is it still making the sound?

Yeah.
 Did you tell Andrew?
Oh, Andrew, it's making a sound-
Here, listen.
O.S.) Guys-
You've got to get really close to hear it.
Andrew takes a tentative step towards the hole.
O.S.) Guys, just don't like- don't push me, or do anything like that- 15.

O.S.) You've got to go closer.
 Like almost inside it.
There's a beat.
We're not going to push you, we just wanna get it on tape.
Another pause. Andrew moves towards the hole. Even with Nightvision, it's completely dark in there. A beat.
O.S.) Do you hear it?
O.S.) ...It's like, whispering?
O.S.) Listen.
O.S.) Singing?

O.S.)
 STEVE (O.S.) Ooooh shiiiiit-
 That is so fucking creepy!
Andrew turns the camera back to Matt and Steve.
O.S.) What is that?
We didn't dig the hole, man. MATT Is the sound on the camera?
O.S.) I don't know, probably...I mean, it's- it's kinda loud, right?
Matt looks to Steve.
Do you guys wanna go in and have a look, or what-
O.S.) No, I don't- I mean, I won't do th-
Steve suddenly goes right into the hole, vanishing into the darkness.
O.S.) Hey, wait-

INT. THE HOLE
 It's almost pitch black, but we can hear Andrew breathing. It's claustrophobic in here. Eerily quiet. The camera searches around, but the walls are tight in here, and low.
O.S.) Hello-

STEVE
 ANDREW (O.S.) (suddenly appearing)
 Aah! I'm right here.
O.S.) Does it go down much deeper, or-
Yeah, really far. It's not a cave, it's like a tunnel- do you hear how loud that's getting?
O.S.) Do you think it could be like wind, or-
Come on, man. Come on.
O.S.) Then what do you think it is? 17.
Huh. I dunno. I don't believe in ghosts.
O.S.) Who said anything about ghosts.

Steve laughs, and turns, going back into the darkness. Andrew follows.
 CUT.
 Matt's face is suddenly in front of the camera.
shouldn't have come in here.
O.S.) Steve's up ahead-
At first I thought this was a solutional cave, but now...I dunno, it's like a lava tube, or something.
O.S.) I don't know what you're talking about.

Look at the walls. Look at how smooth they are. It's just straight shot down, I mean, we must've already gone forty or fifty feet- ANDREW (O.S.) Don't talk about it, you're making me nervous.
 You ever hear of Plato's allegory of the cave?
O.S.) No.

Walking. Darkness. The cave is even tighter now. The light flickers, some kind of electrical disturbance, then goes out completely We hear Andrew curse, fumbling with it, and the nightvision mode comes on.
 CUT.
 The nightvision is scrambling badly.
 All the forms are distorted and then-
O.S.) Can you believe this, this is NUTS-

The nightvision is turned off, revealing Matt. They've come into a larger chamber in the cave, one somehow lit from within; the boys are bathed in a softly pulsing turquoise glow.
 They speak loudly, clearly talking over something we can't hear.
Is that, is that showing up on the camera?
O.S.) I don't wanna film it, it's messing with the camera-

Matt forcibly turns the camera, to reveal Steve standing next to some kind of massive crystalline rock structure growing from, or maybe embedded in, the wall. Around it, and him, float wispy little clouds of light, turquoise and beautiful.
 Steve is clearly entranced, sweeping his hands through the light. STEVE Touch one!
 We see Matt, now also surrounded by the little aurora borealis like clouds, touch one; it comes apart in a silent little explosion of light, beautiful.
O.S.) Guys maybe...maybe we shouldn't...

Andrew is now very near to the crystalline structure; viewed up close, it's almost arachnid, organic somehow. The light clouds have turned yellow. Matt is practically inside the structure, pressing it with his palms.
changes color, see? It must be reacting to the heat, some kind of exothermic reaction-
O.S.) WHAT? I can't hear you over the- you know-
Ready?
O.S.) Rolling, yeah.

Andrew smiles, and points to the suspended ball.
 A trickle of blood comes out of his nose.
Ah, shit.

INT. MATT'S KITCHEN
 Steve is very intently using his newfound powers to make a peanut butter and jelly sandwich, knife shakily moving over bread. The camera is set down on the counter, Matt and Andrew watching.
The trick is to pretend like it's on a plate. You get it on the plate and then you wrap the plate around it, and you can move it however you want.

Yeah?
 Plate's easier for me.

INT. MATT'S ROOM - LATER
 Matt's room is covered in band posters, kind of a mess. Matt's sitting on the floor by a desk, slowly assembling a LEGO set with his telekinetic abilities. Andrew's filming from the bed, where he's laying down.
O.S.) Try to imagine that your squeezing the entire piece in your hand.
That's what I'm doing, it's just, it's hard. You're better at this than me, you do it.
mocking) Really? Andrew points at the lego set, and it flies together.

SAMANTHA (ON PHONE) I know you're over at that guy Matt's house right now, okay, cause they said you didn't show up for soccer practice, but you said the only reason you couldn't come see Bound By Love with me was because of soccer practice, so...You know, I just, I don't know what to think. I feel like we never hang out anymore, you've just been hanging out with those two guys for the last like three weeks, and I...ugh, call me back.
What would you suggest, as like, a course of action for me, here?
Why are you asking US for advice on girls?
O.S.) Because we're his mistress.

INT. STEVE'S CAR - NIGHT
 Steve is driving Andrew home.

new camera's working out for you, that's good. CUT.
 I have to admit, though, I don't understand the filming thing.
O.S.) I told you, it's just my thing for right now.
You don't feel like it's a little weird? Like it puts a barrier between you and everything?
O.S.) Maybe I want a barrier. 24.

Okay.
 I respect that.
CUT.

O.S.) My dad is actually a fireman, or was, I don't know. He got injured like two years ago, and now they just pay him out of, like, insurance. He doesn't do anything, I mean he goes out during the day, but I don't know where. He drinks a lot.
So how do you pay for your shit?

O.S.) I do computer repair, and stuff.
 I actually make a lot of money...
What about your mom.

O.S.) My mom has spinal meningitis, she's- she can't work or anything, and the insurance doesn't cover all of her medicine, so a lot of my money has to go into that. I figure if I get enough on camera, maybe I can, I dunno, get custody of her or something.
Steve mulls this over. CUT.
I've actually been having some problems with my parents, too.
V.O.) Yeah?
They've just been fighting a lot. I think my mom is cheating on my dad.
O.S.) ...That's intense.
Yeah. I know. I'm actually, yeah, I'm sure my mom is cheating on my dad.
O.S.) How-
I just, you know, I saw some stuff. It's weird, you don't think about your parents as like...people, or whatever. I don't know.
The two guys drive in silence.
Don't tell anybody about that-
O.S.) Haha no of course, no way.
Weights, like-
Free-weights, a barbell. I got up to two hundred pounds before I bled.
So it's, yeah, this is my theory- it's like a vagina. Like it's elastic, but if you stretch it too far too quick it'll tear-
O.S.) Ew-
And that's why we're getting better. We're loosening up.
O.S.) Couldn't that metaphor just be for any muscle in the human body, like working out or-

No, I like braingina.
 Braingina is the shiiiiit.
Steve floats up a bunch of chips from his bag, and then rapid fires them into his mouth.
O.S.) I think it's time we took this out of the backyard. Matt and Steve look confused.
Matt and Steve are walking in, Andrew lagging behind filming.
Just stay back a little ways, yeah-
O.S.) I got it.

INT. WAL-MART
 The guys are trying to stay out of sight, looking down an aisle where shoppers stand.
O.S.) That girl, with the cart.

A frumpy woman with a shopping cart suddenly starts pulling on the cart. It suddenly breaks away from her, and goes careening down the aisle by itself, her chasing after it. We can hear the guys cracking up, trying to stay quiet.
O.S.) She's chasing- she's chasing it-

CUT.
 Toy aisle. A toddler is looking at teddy bears.

O.S.) I got this. One of the teddy bears suddenly comes to life, waving at the little boy. The little boy is wowed. The teddy bear suddenly takes off and flies through the air. Again the stifled laughter.
O.S.) Umm... (camera finds the guy) Got him.

EXT. FIELD - SUNSET
 Matt sits on the hood of a car.
 He and Andrew are flinging rocks with their powers.
That was a good one, you got that one far.
O.S.) Hey Matt. (beat) Do you like me?
What? (beat) Sure, yeah. I mean, I didn't, always-
O.S.) Why not?
O.S.) You're an asshole.
See, that's what I'm saying. You're hostile, man.
O.S.) (beat) You know, you...If you hadn't invited me to that party, none of this would've happened.
Yeah.
O.S.) Thanks.

INT. MATT'S CAR - AFTERNOON - WAL-MART PARKING LOT
 None of the guys are visible.
 They're filming a woman park her car.
O.S.) I just always feel like I miss stuff.
O.S.) We were just sitting around throwing rocks, it was nothing.
O.S.) Yeah, until all those chicks showed up.

O.S.) What?
 Shut up.
O.S.) Okay, here we go, here we go.
O.S.) Are you sure there's no one around-

EXT. WAL-MART - PARKING LOT - SHORTLY THEREAFTER
 Matt is sitting against the car with a wad of kleenex on his nose, soaked with blood. Steve is leaning nearby, with a brown paper napkin.

beat) My braingina is having like a...face period. STEVE Yeah, you got some from your ears, too. You've gotta start working it out more, Matt.
 Yeah man, in between my yoga.
O.S.) You just need focus, Matt.
Ha.
O.S.) You're lucky you got the kleenex, I'm stuck with the taco bell wrapper. 31.

INT. MATT'S CAR - MOMENTS LATER
 Matt's driving, Steve's shotgun, Andrew filming from the back. It's raining.
sfucking insane, we just changed her life. We did-
O.S.) Her face, I can't get her face out of my head.
He's like right on your bumper, too.
Matt rolls down the window and gives the signal to pass. More honking.
Can you fucking believe this guy?
He's just some asshole redneck, look at him. He's fucking with us.
They are driving out over a bridge in the woods.
I don't know what to do, what should I-
O.S.) (laughing) Here, take the camera, take the camera.
He hands off the camera to Steve; now we see Andrew sitting in the back seat. Behind him, through the back window, we can see a pick-up truck, honking, way too close.
Abracadabra.

EXT. THE BRIDGE
 The camera is being held loosely at Steve's side; everything's upside-down, and we can't see anything, but can hear the guys are clearly freaked out.
O.S.) What the fuck did you do, man, what the fuck-
O.S.) I didn't mean to, I didn't- 33.
O.S.) Fuck! SHIT! What's wrong with you, look at this, fuck!
O.S.) Is he still in there? If he's in there we have to get him out-
O.S.) Guys, just calm down, I'm sorry, okay-
O.S.) Fuck that, take your fucking camera.

The camera is shoved to Andrew's chest, and lingers there a moment before he brings it up to show a view off the bridge; the truck is already sinking in the water fifteen or so feet below. We tilt up to see Steve taking off his jacket.
O.S.) You don't have to- I mean I can-
INT. MATT'S CAR - MOMENTS LATER Andrew's filming Matt, who looks angry as he drives very quickly.
O.S.) Are you mad?

Steve has the guy on the shore.
 He's dazed and bleeding.
 REDNECK ASSHOLE -what happened, cause...I don't know....my truck, my truck...
O.S.) Is he okay?

Steve just stares directly at Andrew.
 Andrew pans to reveal Matt, on a cell phone.
Yes, just past White Crescent bridge. Down by the- yes-
O.S.) Is that the cops, are you calling the cops?

EXT. STADIUM PARKING LOT - NIGHT
 Matt looks FURIOUS, while Steve just looks uncomfortable and freaked out. Matt's car is parked nearby.
O.S.) I don't see how you guys can be so angry-
O.S.) He was being an asshole- he- I didn't mean to-
Which part of that do you mean, do you mean he was being an asshole, or was it an accident? Was it an accident, Andrew, cause you're the strongest. Listen, with this, we can't fuck around, ever.
O.S.) Then what've we been doing, we've been fucking around-
NOT LIKE THAT, ANDREW. That's- you can't use it on people, or hurt people like- Steve, help me out.
Steve seems lost for words.
O.S.) I-
We need rules, okay. Rule #1, you can't use it on living things. Rule #2, you can't use it when you're angry-
O.S.) You can't just declare rules, you're not- MATT I will fucking declare rules, okay? If we keep going, and getting stronger, we need rules. We need rules. (beat) Damn it Andrew turn off the cam-

INT. MATT'S CAR - STEVE'S STREET
 Andrew's filming Matt from shotgun.

O.S.) Did he say what it was about?
 When he called, what did he say?
Matt's quiet.
O.S.) Are you still mad at me?
I'm not mad.
O.S.) (beat) You seem mad.
Power corrupts, is all I'm saying, man.
What does that even mean?
I just mean that we have to think about things more now, okay? We can't just DO things, we have to think first.
I understand. 37.

EXT. STEVE'S HOUSE - MORNING
 The house is big and nice, somewhat isolated.
 Matt is walking up ahead of Andrew to the door.
O.S.) Hey kids.
Matt stops, and both Matt and Andrew look around.

Steve?
 Where are you?

CUT.
 Steve is atop a power line pole, thirty feet in the air. Andrew and Matt look up at him from the ground.
Just try it, it's easy.
O.S.) That doesn't look easy.
You throw yourself at the ground. You just fall, and catch yourself, and then it's easy, I swear. Don't try to jump or you'll flip yourself. CUT.
Matt hurls himself at the ground, WHAM. Cut, and again, WHAM. Cut, and this time he flips himself BAM WHAM.
O.S.) Let me try-

Andrew throws himself forward.
 And stops, hanging in mid air.
Whoaaaamygod...whoaaa...
It's about aerodynamics! If I put a barrier just in front of me, I can go way faster, cause it cuts wind resistance!
O.S.) I can't hear a damn thing you're saying!

Yeah!
 Definitely!

CUT.
 Steve and Matt are seen flying around, Steve further away, near a large cloud bank. Matt is using his abilities to shape cloud fluff into an M. There is a humming, rattling sound, slightly audible even over the deafening wind.
O.S.) Guys, do you hear that?
Neither of the guys can hear him. Steve however stops in the distance, looking around. He shouts something, inaudible.

O.S.) What?
 I can't hear you!

You saved
 my life, dude! Andrew, you saved
 my fucking life! Is that- Holy shit
 is that the camera?! You AND THE CAMERA!? caught me
exhausted) Y- yeah-
I CAN FUCKING FLY!
Andrew cheers from behind the camera, and Steve laughs.
O.S.) I CAN FLY!

EXT. STEVE'S HOUSE - POOL - NIGHT
 Steve, Matt and Andrew are sitting around the pool outside of Steve's house; empty beers are all around. All three are at least tipsy.
 Matt is up.
Yo Andrew, Andrew, gimme a beat.
O.S.) (flatly) No.

INT. STEVE'S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - LATER
 The guys are in that sleepy mode, now. Andrew's filming, but not really focusing on anything. Matt's sprawled out on a couch opposite him. Steve's in a chair nearby.
But how does she not notice?
Well, I mean, she notices that it's gotten better, definitely. You just have to be subtle.
O.S.) That's so cool. Doesn't that count as breaking rule #1 though?
Pfff, technicalities. MATT Man, I haven't had sex since like...a year.
O.S.) I haven't had sex since ever.
Later. Drowsier. Light's coming from the TV. The camera is laying haphazardly on Andrew. They're all nearly asleep, drowsy drunk.
Hey guys, are you up?
Yeah.
O.S.) YO.
Today was like...the best day of my life. Like I was thinking and I can't think of any day I liked better than today.
There's a silence.
O.S.) Yeah.

EXT. HAVEN HILLS FARM - THE CLEARING - MORNING
 Time has passed; maybe two weeks. It's early. The hole is clearly caved in; the entire area is slumped downwards.
Well that answers that.
O.S.) I feel stupid now that I was scared to come back.

INT. DINER - MORNING
 Steve is filming Andrew and Matt across the table.
O.S.) I'm live on scene here with Matt and Andrew, boys, can you tell us what's happening?
Well, uh, it would appear that a Virgin Mary has appeared in the maple syrup on a young local man's pancake.
In a second it will, wait...
Matt focuses, and we see his syrup form a Virgin Mary.
O.S.) That is, that is some very uh, definitely supernatural shit, going on there.
CUT.
Well, when I was really little, I wanted to be police officer.
O.S.) Oh, no shit?
Fuck you, pig.
